subject: Product Management
title: WebTV: Too Early for Streaming
description: A short lesson to help you apply WebTV: Too Early for Streaming.

hook.text: In 1996, WebTV put the internet on your television. The idea was perfect. The timing was 15 years too early.

buildup.text: Steve Perlman built a set-top box that connected TVs to the web using dial-up modems. Microsoft bought it for $425 million.

discovery.text: Websites in 1996 were designed for computer monitors. On a TV from across the room, text was unreadable.

twist.text: Dial-up was too slow for any meaningful streaming. Loading a single web page took 30 seconds. Video was impossible.

climax.text: WebTV was Netflix, Roku, and Apple TV — a decade before broadband made them possible. Right idea, wrong era.

punchline.text: Being too early is the same as being wrong — until it isn't.

quiz.question: Why did WebTV fail despite having the right concept?
options: Infrastructure wasn't ready — dial-up was too slow and websites weren't designed for TVs
The technology didn't work at all
